name: obsessive-compulsive
description: Studies of obsessive-compulsive traits or disorders including OCD, body dysmorphic disorder, trichotillomania, and related conditions. Not currently represented in existing projects.

name: family
description: Studies focused on family relationships including child-parent dynamics, spousal/partner relationships, and caregiver studies. Not currently represented in existing projects.

name: neurodevelopmental
description: Studies of neurodevelopmental traits or disorders including autism spectrum disorder, ADHD, learning disorders, intellectual disabilities, communication disorders, and Tourettes syndrome.

name: adolescent
description: Studies focused on adolescents ages 12-17, or populations explicitly labeled as adolescent. Includes clinical adolescents in outpatient mental health treatment.

name: stress-trauma
description: Studies of stress and trauma-related traits or disorders including PTSD, acute stress disorder, adjustment disorders, and elevated perceived stress (PSS). Includes studies of college students with stress.

name: bipolar
description: Studies of bipolar traits or disorders including Bipolar I, Bipolar II, and cyclothymia. Includes mood disorder classification and clustering studies.

name: depressive
description: Studies of depressive traits or disorders including major depressive disorder (MDD), persistent depressive disorder, postpartum/perinatal depression, and treatment-resistant depression. The most commonly studied condition in mindLAMP research.

name: child
description: Studies focused on children ages 0-11. Not currently represented in existing mindLAMP projects (studies typically begin at age 12+).

name: anxiety
description: Studies of anxiety traits or disorders including generalized anxiety disorder (GAD), social anxiety, phobias, and panic disorder. Often studied alongside depression in comorbid populations.

name: sex-gender-sexuality
description: Studies focused on sex, gender, or sexuality-related topics including LGBTQ+ populations, OBGYN/reproductive health, menstrual-related conditions, pregnancy/perinatal populations, and gendered anatomy considerations.

name: military-veteran
description: Studies focused on veterans or active military personnel. Includes studies of homeless veterans with serious mental illness.

name: neurocognitive
description: Studies of neurocognitive traits or disorders including Alzheimers disease, Parkinsons disease, Huntingtons disease, mild cognitive impairment (MCI), and dementia. Also includes studies using cognitive games/training for assessment or intervention (e.g., Jewels/Trails, Spatial Span, N-Back, cognitive training apps).

name: eating
description: Studies of eating traits or disorders including anorexia nervosa, bulimia nervosa, binge eating disorder, and related conditions. Not currently represented in existing projects.

name: psychotic
description: Studies of psychotic traits or disorders including schizophrenia, schizoaffective disorder, clinical high risk for psychosis (CHR-p), first episode psychosis (FEP), and hallucinations. Includes relapse prediction and early intervention studies.

name: addictive
description: Studies of addictive behaviors including substance use disorders (alcohol, drugs) and behavioral addictions such as problematic internet use (PIU), social media addiction, and smartphone/screen addiction.

name: low-income
description: Studies focused on underserved, low-income, or resource-limited populations. Includes rural health initiatives and populations with limited healthcare access.

name: older-adult
description: Studies focused on older adults, typically ages 60 and above. Includes studies of cognitive aging, dementia, and digital health feasibility in seniors.

name: sleep
description: Studies of sleep traits or disorders including insomnia, sleep-wake disorders, and circadian rhythm disruptions. Note: Many studies measure sleep as a passive feature but are not focused on sleep disorders specifically.

name: personality
description: Studies focused on personality traits or personality disorders (e.g., borderline, narcissistic, antisocial personality disorders). Not currently represented in existing projects.

name: young-adult
description: Studies focused on young adults ages 18-25, college students, or populations explicitly labeled as young adult. The most common demographic in mindLAMP research.

name: race-ethnicity-language
description: Studies focused on specific racial, ethnic, or linguistic populations, cultural factors in mental health, or international/global mental health collaborations. Includes research conducted in non-US settings (India, Kenya, China, Bulgaria, etc.) and multi-national consortia.
